channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: NVIDIA Stock Climbs 1.2% to $200 as AI Demand Fuels Continued Momentum in Chip Sector
item.description: NVIDIA Corp. shares rose more than 1% Friday, climbing to $200.67 in midday trading on the Nasdaq as investors continued to bet on the company's dominance in artificial intelligence infrastructure, even amid broader market volatility and upcoming supplier ear… [Intel, Nvidia]

item.title: Meta's AI spending spree is helping make its Quest headsets more expensive
item.description: Prices for "critical components" are surging because of massive data center investments. [Meta]

item.title: 5 AI Stocks to Buy from ASX 200 in 2026 Offer Strong Exposure to Data Centers and Tech Infrastructure
item.description: SYDNEY — As artificial intelligence continues to reshape global industries in 2026, investors seeking exposure within the ASX 200 have turned their attention to Australian companies providing the critical infrastructure, data solutions and software platforms … [Intel]

item.title: Apollo's president warns the AI spending boom may not pay off for investors
item.description: US data centers alone could require $5 trillion to $6 trillion over the next five years, Jim Zelter estimates.

item.title: Permitting Hurdles and Labor Shortages Threaten AI Data Center Timelines
item.description: Almost 40% of U.S. data center projects are at risk of falling behind schedule, the Financial Times reported Thursday (April 16), citing data from satellite and artificial intelligence (AI) analytics group SynMax. The company also found that 60% of projects s… [Intel]

item.title: TSMC to expand 3nm chip production in Taiwan, US and Japan as AI demand surges
item.description: TSMC is significantly boosting its 3-nanometer chip production worldwide. This expansion is driven by a surge in demand for artificial intelligence. New facilities are being built and upgraded in Taiwan, the United States, and Japan. This move aims to solidif… [TSMC, Intel]

item.title: TSMC expects its 2026 sales to grow more than 30%
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. expects significant growth in 2026. Strong demand for artificial intelligence applications will drive sales up by more than 30 percent. The company is investing heavily in advanced chip manufacturing. This strategic move… [TSMC, Intel]

item.title: Iconiq, go-to wealth adviser for tech’s elite, is putting billions into AI
item.description: Iconiq is expanding aggressively into venture capital, investing billions in AI startups like Anthropic. Leveraging elite global networks, it connects founders with powerful investors, achieving strong returns while navigating risks, conflicts of interest, an… [Anthropic]

item.title: $16.92 Bn Digital Map Market Forecasts, 2026-2034 by Type, Component, End Use, Countries and Company Analysis
item.description: The digital map market is projected to reach $16.92 billion by 2034, growing at a 10.97% CAGR from 2026, driven by increasing demand for location-based services, smart city initiatives, and autonomous vehicles. Key advancements in AI, IoT, and cloud computing…

item.title: Sustainable Green Team (OTC: SGTM) Launches Restore.Earth Network. The World's First Multi-Channel Broadcast Platform Combining Live Verified Environmental Events, Outdoor Sports, Conservation Gaming, and Professional Programming — Distributed Across 32 FAST TV Platforms With Potential Reach Into 3 Billion Connected Households. Launching Q2 2026.
item.description: 57.9 Million American Anglers. 220 Million Worldwide. 169 Pending Patent Applications. 850,000+ Lines of Code. Testnet Complete. Mainnet Live. The Outdoor World Has Never Seen Anything Like This.

item.title: Maase Inc Stock Surges 32% to $9.84 on AI Acquisition Momentum and Market Enthusiasm
item.description: Shares of Maase Inc. skyrocketed more than 31% Friday, climbing to $9.84 in midday trading on the Nasdaq as investors piled into the small-cap stock following its recent strategic shift toward becoming a full-stack artificial intelligence player through the c… [Intel]

item.title: AXT Inc Stock Soars 30% to $81.78 on AI Hype but Faces Analyst Warnings to Sell in 2026
item.description: NEW YORK — AXT Inc. shares exploded higher Thursday, surging nearly 30% to close at $81.78 after a volatile session that saw the stock swing from $59.30 to an intraday high of $82.19, as momentum traders piled into the compound semiconductor maker amid broade…

item.title: Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects — mounting delays are already costing AI hyperscalers billions
item.description: Local communities all over America and the world are pushing back on the explosive growth of AI data center projects. Through local courts, community action, and intense meetings with politicians, the general public are forcing the cancellation of major data …

item.title: Meta Stock 2026: Buy or Sell? Wall Street Says Strong Buy as AI and Ads Fuel Rally Toward $800+
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. — Meta Platforms Inc. stock has delivered solid gains in early 2026, closing at $676.87 on April 16 after a strong week that saw shares climb more than 7 percent in a single session earlier in the month. [Meta]

item.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
item.description: The Trump administration has spent nearly two months fighting with AI company Anthropic. It's dubbed the company a "RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY" full of "Leftwing nut jobs" and a menace to national security. But some of the ice may reportedly be melting betwee… [Anthropic]

item.title: White House meets AI firm Anthropic amid political tensions, Pentagon dispute
item.description: Trump administration reconsiders Anthropic ban as CEO meets White House officials to discuss Mythos Preview, a powerful new AI cybersecurity model. [Anthropic]

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei Heads to White House Hoping to End Pentagon AI Fight
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei will meet with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles today as the AI giant seeks to end its ongoing conflict with the Pentagon over government use of its technology. The post Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei Heads to White House Hoping t… [Anthropic]

item.title: Canva becomes the design layer inside Claude with new Anthropic partnership
item.description: In short: Canva and Anthropic have launched Claude Design, a new Anthropic Labs product powered by Claude Opus 4.7 that uses Canva’s Design Engine to generate fully editable, on-brand visuals from text descriptions. The announcement coincides with Canva AI 2.… [Anthropic]

item.title: India’s AI value paradox
item.description: While India is becoming a key location for AI infrastructure, it is still building depth in creating the technologies that run on it

item.title: Here's who is spending money on AI subscriptions, and how much they cost
item.description: Households are starting to make room in their budgets for spending on generative AI subscriptions, new data shows.

item.title: 5G Security Market Analysis Report 2026: $23.1 Bn Opportunities, Trends, Competitive Landscape, Strategies, and Forecasts, 2020-2025, 2025-2030F, 2035F
item.description: Key market opportunities lie in increasing adoption of private 5G networks, high demand for secure IoT connectivity, smart city and industrial 5G applications, and regulatory focus on telecom security. Growth is driven by rising cyber-attacks, requiring advan…

item.title: Uber launches $5 doorstep return pickups for Uber Eats purchases across 5,000 US cities
item.description: Uber has launched a service that sends a gig worker to your door to collect items you want to return to a retailer, for $5 a pickup. The feature, called Return a Package, is available in the Uber Eats app across nearly 5,000 US cities and works with nine reta… [Uber]

item.title: 8 Ways to Elevate Your Brand as a Creator or Entrepreneur (& Close the Pay Gap)
item.description: While many are still skeptical, the global creator economy is expected to reach $1.18 trillion USD by 2032. And for minority creators and entrepreneurs from underrepresented groups, this moment is especially significant.

item.title: I Wore the Whoop Band and the Apple Watch for Months and Found the Best Fit
item.description: The Whoop band won't tell you the time, but it might change the way you work out. Here's who should wear which. [Apple]

item.title: Strong ASML, TSMC forecasts signal AI spending boom is intact
item.description: April 16 : Strong forecasts from ASML and TSMC this week point to another quarter of hefty spending by American cloud-computing giants as they race to secure advanced chips needed for their artificial intelligence build-outs.The results suggest that demand st… [TSMC, Intel]

item.title: Alphabet Stock Dips Slightly to $333.97 as Investors Await Q1 Earnings and Massive AI Spending
item.description: NEW YORK — Alphabet Inc. Class C shares traded modestly lower in early morning action Thursday, slipping about 0.15 percent to around $333.97 as Wall Street digested recent gains and prepared for the tech giant's first-quarter 2026 earnings report later this … [Alphabet]

item.title: Tesla Stock Dips to $386 as Post-Surge Profit-Taking Hits Ahead of Q1 Earnings
item.description: NEW YORK — Tesla Inc. shares pulled back in early trading Thursday, falling about 1.5 percent to around $386 after a sharp 7.6 percent surge the previous day, as investors locked in gains from Elon Musk's latest AI and chip updates while awaiting the electric… [Tesla]

item.title: Why I Can’t Stop Buying Amazon Stock
item.description: Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has delivered accelerating numbers, a widening moat, and a valuation that makes sense for what this company is becoming. I can’t stop... [Amazon]

item.title: Petrochemicals Market Size to Reach USD 1,263.95 Billion By 2035
item.description: The petrochemicals market is projected to grow from USD 757.73 billion in 2026 to USD 1,263.95 bn by 2035, at a CAGR of 5.85% during the forecast period....
